name: User Access Review
description: User access to systems and data is periodically reviewed to ensure access remains appropriate and aligned with each individual's role. User access reviews reduce the risk of unauthorised or excessive access by confirming that only authorised individuals retain access and that access is revoked when no longer required.

attributes.text: Access reviews are performed on a periodic basis (e.g. quarterly)
testable_criteria: Evidence shows an access review was performed for the in-scope system(s) during the audit period.
The frequency of reviews observed matches the stated cadence (e.g. quarterly = at least one review per quarter within the audit period).
The review was completed within the defined period (no missed or skipped cycles).
The date of review completion is documented on the evidence.

attributes.text: Access is reviewed and approved by an appropriate system or data owner
testable_criteria: The reviewer(s) named on the evidence are identified as the system owner or data owner for the in-scope system.
The reviewer is independent of the users whose access they are reviewing (i.e. did not review their own access).
Documented approval (sign-off, ticket, signature, or equivalent) from the reviewer is present on the evidence.
The list of users reviewed reconciles to the complete population of users with access to the system at the time of review.

attributes.text: Inappropriate or excessive access identified during the review is remediated in a timely manner
testable_criteria: Any access flagged as inappropriate or excessive during the review is documented with a remediation action.
For each flagged item, evidence of remediation (e.g. access removal ticket, screenshot of revoked access) is retained.
Remediation occurred after the review identification date and can be tied back to the specific review finding.
Post-remediation, the flagged user no longer has the inappropriate/excessive access in the system.
